title: s13-d1 RSI(2) bi-directional MNQ.c.0 — DRAFT L1-gap-closure addendum (addendum-only; not a competing DRAFT; not a SEAL; carries L1 discount framework into the chain before any SEAL)
report_kind: Sealed addendum to s13-d1 Tier-N spec DRAFT 8fcefaf. Applies the L1 epistemic discount framework from L1-discount memo 2869945 (seal e41690d6...) to the T1 RSI(2) ~50-65 trades/year estimate. Classifies the source, presents formal 2x/3x discount tables, discloses the 3x scenario explicitly, renames the DRAFT's 'REC1-equivalent' as REC1_T1_BINDING_K9_DISCLOSURE, mandates byte-equivalent carry through future SEAL/P1/P2/P3/P4/P6/P6.5/P7/P10/P11. No code, no build, no execution, no signal, no fetch, no CSV read.
operator_authorization_phrase: Authorize a short S13-D1 L1-gap-closure addendum only

addendum_status_attestation.addendum_role: Attaches a binding L1-discount framework + REC1_T1 disclosure to the s13-d1 DRAFT at 8fcefaf. The DRAFT remains the source of truth for DA1-DA14 and the locked-at-PLAN mechanic parameters; this addendum supplements but does NOT supersede or replace it. A future SEAL of s13-d1 shall carry both 8fcefaf DRAFT content AND this addendum's REC1_T1 disclosure byte-equivalent.

source_classification_for_t1_50_to_65_pe.estimate_as_quoted_from_5e57984_plan_sec: ~50-65 trades/year (long+short bi-directional; daily bars on MNQ.c.0)
evidence_provided_in_plan: Larry Connors' published RSI(2) research suggesting 50-100 entries/year on liquid equity indices (name-checked; NO specific paper / edition / URL / page reference provided)
Bi-directional symmetric thresholds typically each contribute roughly equally to total trade count
MNQ.c.0 vol profile is broadly similar to NQ.c.0 (scaled-down notional)
source_classification_for_t1_50_to_65_pe.operator_decision_required: If operator has access to a specific Connors RSI(2) citation that quantifies trade rates on daily-bar liquid futures indices (e.g., Connors & Alvarez 'Short-Term Trading Strategies That Work' specific chapter and table reference), the source class upgrades to PUBLISHED_LITERATURE and L1 discount drops to 1.5x. If no specific citation is available, the conservative classification is HEURISTIC_ESTIMATE_L1_DISCOUNT_APPLIES and full 2-3x discount scenarios are the binding ones for the lifecycle decision.
source_classification_for_t1_50_to_65_pe.rationale_for_default: Per the L1-discount memo at 2869945, partial citations without specific paper/edition/URL anchoring default to HEURISTIC. The Connors name-check in 5e57984 PLAN §9.2 is informative but not a verifiable citation. Operator may upgrade to PUBLISHED_LITERATURE with a specific citation.

central.label: central_50_to_65_per_year
central.likely_terminal_risk: OOS K9 CLEARS_BORDERLINE at low (50.0/y matches floor exactly), CLEARS_BORDERLINE at mid (1.15x), CLEARS_BORDERLINE at high (1.30x). Likely terminal risk: PASS_BUT_THIN_OOS_MARGIN if observed rate matches central estimate; INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE if observed rate < 50/y.

2x_discount.label: 2x_l1_discount_25_to_32_5_per_year
2x_discount.likely_terminal_risk: OOS K9 FAILS across the entire range (25-32.5/y < 50/y floor). IS K9 clears borderline at high (32.5/y) and mid (28.75/y); fails-borderline at low (25/y < 21.74/y floor only barely clears at 1.15x). Likely terminal risk: INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE_AT_OOS_K9_FIRED analogous to s12-d1 PARK pattern. PARK is expected terminal outcome.

3x_discount.label: 3x_l1_discount_16_7_to_21_7_per_year
3x_discount.likely_terminal_risk: OOS K9 FAILS SEVERELY across entire range (16.7-21.7/y vs 50/y floor; 0.33-0.43x). IS K9 FAILS at low (16.7/y < 21.74/y) and mid (19.2/y); marginally clears at high (21.7/y - essentially AT_FLOOR_INDETERMINATE). Likely terminal risk: BOTH IS AND OOS K9 FIRED. PARK at IS without even reaching P10. Direct replication of s12-d1 PARK pattern.

formal_l1_discount_table_t1_rsi_2_bidir_.summary: Under HEURISTIC_ESTIMATE_L1_DISCOUNT_APPLIES classification, the 2x discount scenario shows OOS K9 FAILS across the entire 25-32.5/y range. The 3x discount scenario shows BOTH IS K9 and OOS K9 FAIL. Only the central scenario (50-65/y) clears OOS K9, and only borderline at the low end (50/y exactly matches the 50/y floor).

explicit_3x_discount_scenario_disclosure.purpose: The s13-d1 PLAN/DRAFT (5e57984 + 8fcefaf) do NOT disclose the 3x discount scenario at all. This addendum makes it explicit and binding.
explicit_3x_discount_scenario_disclosure.is_k9_outcome_at_3x: FAILS at low (16.7/y < 21.74/y floor) and mid (19.2/y < 21.74/y). AT_FLOOR_INDETERMINATE at high (21.7/y barely matches 21.74/y floor).
explicit_3x_discount_scenario_disclosure.oos_k9_outcome_at_3x: FAILS SEVERELY across entire range. Margins 0.33 / 0.38 / 0.43 vs 1.0 floor. This is the worst case where s13-d1 fails K9 at BOTH windows.
explicit_3x_discount_scenario_disclosure.lifecycle_implication: Under 3x discount, s13-d1 would PARK at IS analogous to s12-d1 (INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE at IS K9 fire), without even reaching P10 OOS. The P6 IS diagnostic would document closed_trades 76-100 (vs K9=100 required), short by 0-24 trades. Net PnL outcome would be informational only and would NOT override K9 verdict per LESSON_B006_002_002.
explicit_3x_discount_scenario_disclosure.structural_parallel_to_s12_d1: s12-d1 PARK at ecbd001 was PARKED_SAFE_BUT_INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE_AT_IS with 48 closed trades vs K9=100 (48% of floor). Under 3x L1 discount, s13-d1 mid-scenario implies 4.6 * 19.2 = 88.3 closed trades over IS (88% of floor). The s12-d1 PARK pattern would repeat under this scenario, though with a less severe shortfall. Per L1-memo guidance and the K9 inviolacy principle, this would be a sealed PARK verdict.

rec1_t1_binding_k9_disclosure.renaming_directive: The DRAFT 8fcefaf §9.4 (PLAN) and §10.2 (DRAFT) reference a 'REC1-equivalent disclosure for s13-d1' specifying a 25/y tripwire. Per this addendum, that disclosure is FORMALLY RENAMED and REINFORCED as REC1_T1_BINDING_K9_DISCLOSURE with the full L1 discount framework attached.
rec1_t1_binding_k9_disclosure.rec1_t1_binding_text: REC1_T1 (binding): Under the L1 epistemic-discount framework (memo 2869945 seal e41690d6b1ecd824fa5521525f64cdd13e3e558c6b8a93123e4a83f1d515c5b3), the T1 RSI(2) bi-directional MNQ.c.0 trade-rate estimate of 50-65 trades/year is classified as HEURISTIC_ESTIMATE_L1_DISCOUNT_APPLIES pending operator-supplied specific citation or hand-count validation. At the 2x conservative discount (25-32.5 trades/year), OOS K9 (>= 50/y over 2.0y = 100 trades) FIRES; expected OOS verdict is OOS_INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE or PARKED_SAFE_BUT_OOS_INDETERMINATE_K9_FIRED. At the 3x conservative discount (16.7-21.7 trades/year), BOTH IS K9 and OOS K9 fire; expected IS verdict is INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE analogous to s12-d1 PARK at ecbd001 (48 closed trades < K9=100). The chain shall NOT relax K9 at any phase. The 25/y tripwire identified in DRAFT 8fcefaf §10.2 is the 2x discount low-end and remains the parking trigger. Pursuing s13-d1 without independent source validation of the 50-65/y estimate accepts the structural likelihood of OOS PARK (under 2x discount) or IS PARK (under 3x discount) outcomes analogous to s12-d1.

embedded_discount_reconciliation.issue_statement: The 5e57984 PLAN §9.2 derives the 50-65/y central estimate from Connors' 50-100/y long-only equity-index rate, doubled approximately for bi-directional (yielding 100-200/y), and then implicitly reduced to 50-65/y for MNQ.c.0 futures. The reduction step from 100-200/y (Connors-doubled) to 50-65/y (PLAN central) is unacknowledged in the PLAN but corresponds to a ~2-3x cross-instrument extrapolation discount (equity-indices to MNQ futures).
embedded_discount_reconciliation.implication_for_layered_l1_discount: If the 50-65/y central is interpreted as already-L1-2x-discounted from Connors-doubled 100-200/y, then applying ANOTHER 2x L1 discount (yielding 25-32.5/y) is a COMPOUND 4x discount from Connors baseline. If interpreted as the raw central estimate with no embedded discount, then the 2x and 3x L1 discounts are first-application and 25-32.5/y is the appropriate conservative scenario.

two_possible_readings.interpretation: 50-65/y is the raw central estimate; L1 2x and 3x discounts are first-application; 25-32.5/y (2x) and 16.7-21.7/y (3x) are conservative scenarios. This is the assumption underlying this addendum's formal L1 discount table.
two_possible_readings.implication: Standard L1 framework applies. 2x = 25-32.5/y FAILS OOS K9. 3x = 16.7-21.7/y FAILS both K9 windows.

two_possible_readings.interpretation: 50-65/y already silently incorporates a 2-3x cross-instrument extrapolation discount from Connors-doubled 100-200/y. Further L1 discount would be COMPOUND with the embedded discount and potentially overconservative.
two_possible_readings.implication: If READING_B is correct, the formal 2x L1 discount above is actually a 4x compound discount from Connors baseline, and the 3x is 6x compound. The honest central estimate under READING_B might be closer to 50-65/y itself (already L1-aware by construction), with 2x/3x further-discount scenarios representing stress tests rather than expected ranges.

embedded_discount_reconciliation.resolution_required_before_seal: Operator (or future SEAL authoring turn) must explicitly choose READING_A or READING_B and document the choice in the s13-d1 SEAL. Either reading is defensible; the absence of a documented choice is the gap this addendum surfaces.
embedded_discount_reconciliation.rationale_for_default_reading_a: The PLAN does not explicitly document an embedded discount; treating the central estimate as discount-free and applying L1 on top is the more conservative interpretation. Operator may revise to READING_B with a specific Connors citation that quantifies the equity-to-futures translation.

final_status_list: l1_3x_16_7_to_21_7: fails BOTH IS and OOS K9 (parallel to s12-d1 PARK)
embedded_discount_issue: surfaced; reading A or B operator choice required at SEAL
seal_method: LESSON_HUNTER_004 canonical roundtrip (json.dumps sort_keys=True separators=',:' ensure_ascii=False default=str EXCLUDING report_seal_sha256 + seal_method)
